title: Goeppertia zebrina
intro: Goeppertia zebrina, also known as Calathea zebrina, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Goeppertia zebrina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
